prompt:
We're building a blackjack trainer and the piece I need first is the dealer engine — completely deterministic so QA can replay a session card for card. Go module go-blackjack, package blackjack at the root, stdlib only. No RNG anywhere: cards come off a fixture shoe in order, player decisions come from a script, and the engine's only job is to referee the house rules below EXACTLY and narrate what happened.

Inputs. ParseShoe(s string) ([]string, error): the shoe is whitespace-separated cards, rank A23456789TJQK + suit HDCS (so "TH", "AS"; never "10H"), '#' starts a comment to end of line, unknown tokens are errors; shoes span multiple decks so repeats are fine. HandValue(cards []string) (total int, soft bool, err error): best total, soft=true when an ace is currently counted as 11. Run(shoe, script string, rounds int) (string, error): plays that many rounds (error if < 1), drawing player actions (hit/stand/double/split, same comment rules) from the script stream across rounds; leftover actions when the last round ends are an error, running out mid-decision is an error, and any error returns an empty transcript.

House rules, all pinned by tests: flat 10 bet per hand, bankroll starts at 100. Deal order: player, dealer up, player, dealer hole. A two-card 21 on the original deal is a natural — dealer natural loses the round immediately (-10), player natural pays 3:2 (+15), both is a push, and natural rounds consume NO script actions. Otherwise the player acts: hit draws; stand ends the hand; double only on exactly two cards (double the bet, one card, done); split only on the ORIGINAL two-card hand when both cards have equal value (ten-J-Q-K all count ten) — the two hands each immediately draw one card (first hand first), then are played in order; a split hand may double but may NEVER split again (that's a scripting error), and split aces get their one card and stand automatically. Any hand reaching 21 stops taking actions on its own. A 21 made after a split is NOT a natural. Dealer: reveals the hole card, then hits until 17 or more and STANDS on soft 17 — and if every player hand busted the dealer reveals but draws nothing. Settlement per hand: bust loses its bet, otherwise beat the dealer's total (or dealer bust) to win the bet, tie pushes.

The transcript (returned string, every line \n-terminated) is pinned byte-for-byte by the tests, grammar by example: '== round 1 ==' / 'player: TH QD (20)' / 'dealer: AH ??' / action lines 'hit: 4S (11)', 'stand: (20)', 'double: QC (21)' / split rounds add 'split: 8C | 8D', 'hand 1: 8C 3S (11)', 'hand 2: 8D 2D (10)' before each hand's actions / reveal 'dealer: AH 6C (soft 17)' then 'dealer hit: 9D (24 bust)' per draw / results 'result: win +10', 'result: lose -10', 'result: push +0', 'result: blackjack +15', 'result: dealer blackjack -10', or per-hand 'result hand 1: ...' when split / 'bankroll: 110' closes the round. Values print as '20', 'soft 17', '24 bust', and a natural two-card deal prints '(blackjack)'.

testdata/ has the QA shoes; blackjack_test.go is the acceptance contract and diffs whole transcripts, so every soft-17 stand, split payout and bankroll line matters. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass; don't touch the test file or the shoes.
